08-03.text: Jesus came to a town of Samaria called Sychar... Jacob's well was there. Jesus said to the woman: 'If you knew the gift of God and who is saying to you, "Give me a drink," you would have asked him and he would have given you living water.'
08-03.meditation: Jesus is the only one who can truly satisfy the deepest thirst of our hearts.
08-03.oratio: Lord, I am thirsty. Give me this Living Water so that I may never seek satisfaction in things that do not last.
08-03.contemplatio: Imagine yourself at the well with Jesus. Let Him look at you and offer you the water of eternal life.

09-03.text: Jesus said: 'Amen, I say to you, no prophet is accepted in his own native place.' When the people heard this, they were filled with fury. They led him to the brow of the hill to hurl him down, but he passed through the midst of them and went away.
09-03.meditation: Do I recognize God's voice in the ordinary people around me, or do I reject the truth when it comes from someone familiar?
09-03.oratio: Lord, remove the scales of pride from my eyes that prevent me from seeing Your work in my daily life.
09-03.contemplatio: Visualize Jesus calmly walking through the crowd. Rest in His peace, which no worldly rejection can destroy.

10-03.text: Peter asked: 'Lord, if my brother sins against me, how often must I forgive? As many as seven times?' Jesus answered, 'I say to you, not seven times but seventy-seven times.'
10-03.meditation: Forgiveness is not a calculation; it is a reflection of the mercy we have already received from God.
10-03.oratio: Heavenly Father, soften my heart toward those who have hurt me, that I may reflect Your love through forgiveness.
10-03.contemplatio: Consider the vast debt God has cleared for you. Let that gratitude flow out toward others.

11-03.text: Jesus said: 'Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be handed over to be crucified.' Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee asked: 'Command that these two sons of mine sit, one at your right and one at your left, in your kingdom.'
11-03.meditation: True greatness in the Kingdom of God is found in service, not in status. To lead is to serve.
11-03.oratio: Lord, grant me a servant's heart. May I seek not to be served, but to serve my brothers and sisters with love.
11-03.contemplatio: Look at Jesus, the King who washes feet. Stay in silence contemplating His humility.
